title: Anthropic's Biggest Week: Sonnet 5 Out, Fable Restored
meta: Claude Sonnet 5 launched June 30 as a near-Opus agentic model at Sonnet prices — and Fable 5 returned globally after an 18-day export-control suspension. Here's what changed for every Claude user.
bodyHtml:
On June 30, two things happened at once at Anthropic. The US Department of Commerce lifted the export controls that had locked Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 behind a compliance wall for 18 days. And Anthropic shipped Claude Sonnet 5 — now the default model for every Free and Pro subscriber — as if to say: the company is back in full force.

The Fable saga deserves a rewind. On June 12, Amazon researchers documented a jailbreak that bypassed Fable 5's safety guardrails, prompting the model to identify software vulnerabilities and produce working exploit code. Amazon CEO Andy Jassy flagged it to US officials. Within hours, the federal government issued a national-security export control directive: no foreign national — including Anthropic's own employees — could access Fable 5 or Mythos 5. The models went dark.

Eighteen days later, after Anthropic developed a new automated safety classifier in coordination with Amazon and federal officials, the directive was lifted. Fable 5 returns globally from July 1. Cloud platform rollout follows in phases.

But for most everyday Claude users, Sonnet 5 is the bigger story. Anthropic calls it its most agentic Sonnet yet, and that's not marketing noise. Sonnet 5 scores 63.2% on an agentic coding benchmark — up from Sonnet 4.6's 58.1% and within striking distance of Opus 4.8's 69.2%. It can plan, browse the web, run terminals, and complete multi-step tasks that, months ago, required the most expensive models available. On some knowledge-work benchmarks, it outperforms Opus 4.8 outright.

The practical upshot: if you're using Claude on any plan today, your default is now meaningfully smarter at independent work — with no setting changes required.

Pricing reinforces the value case. Through August 31, Sonnet 5 runs at $2 per million input tokens and $10 per million output — undercutting OpenAI's GPT-5.5 and Google's Gemini 3.1 Pro at launch. After August 31, those rates rise to $3/$15, but developers building agentic pipelines today are getting near-Opus performance at a fraction of the historical cost.

Sonnet 5 ships with real-time cyber safeguards on by default — the same protections applied to Opus 4.7 and 4.8 — and shows lower rates of sycophancy and hallucination than its predecessor. Whether that safety story holds as more agentic deployments go live will be worth watching.

If you want to put Sonnet 5's new capabilities to work, ClaudeCraft has done-for-you skill bundles built to help you get more out of Claude — from everyday productivity to creative writing to job searching. Browse what's available at https://claudecraft.ca.
sourcesHtml:
• TechCrunch: Anthropic launches Claude Sonnet 5 as a cheaper way to run agents (June 30, 2026)
  • CNBC: Anthropic says Trump admin has lifted export controls on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 (June 30, 2026)
  • gHacks: Anthropic Releases Claude Sonnet 5 With Near-Opus Performance, Restores Fable 5 and Mythos 5 (July 1, 2026)
  • CoinDesk: Anthropic restores AI models Fable, Mythos after the U.S. lifts export controls (July 1, 2026)
  • Nerova AI: Claude Sonnet 5 Launches — Pricing, Availability, and What It Means for AI Agents
